title: नारायण कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नारायण कवचम् ॥

॥ अथ श्रीमद्भागवतान्तर्गतं नारायणकवचम् ॥

राजोवाच —
यया गुप्तः सहस्राक्षः सवाहान् रिपुसैनिकान्।
क्रीडन्निव विनिर्जित्य त्रिलोक्या बुभुजे श्रियम्॥ १॥

भगवंस्तन्ममाख्याहि वर्म नारायणात्मकम्।
यथाऽऽततायिनः शत्रून् येन गुप्तोऽजयन्मृधे॥ २॥

श्रीविश्वरूप उवाच —
धौताङ्घ्रिपाणिराचम्य सपवित्र उदङ्मुखः।
कृतस्वाङ्गकरन्यासो मन्त्राभ्यां वाग्यतः शुचिः॥ ३॥

नारायणपरं वर्म सन्नह्येद्भय आगते।
पादयोर्जानुनोरूर्वोरुदरे हृद्यथोरसि॥ ४॥

मुखे शिरस्यानुपूर्व्यादोंकारादीनि विन्यसेत्।
ॐ नमो नारायणायेति विपर्ययमथापि वा॥ ५॥

करन्यासं ततः कुर्याद्द्वादशाक्षरविद्यया।
प्रणवादियकारान्तमङ्गुल्यङ्गुष्ठपर्वसु॥ ६॥

न्यसेद्धृदय ओंकारं विकारमनु मूर्धनि।
षकारं तु भ्रुवोर्मध्ये णकारं शिखया दिशेत्॥ ७॥

वेकारं नेत्रयोर्युञ्ज्यान्नकारं सर्वसन्धिषु।
मकारमस्त्रमुद्दिश्य मन्त्रमूर्तिर्भवेद्बुधः॥ ८॥

सविसर्गं फडन्तं तत् सर्वदिक्षु विनिर्दिशेत्।
ॐ विष्णवे नम इति ॥ ९॥

आत्मानं परमं ध्यायेद्ध्येयं षट्शक्तिभिर्युतम्।
विद्यातेजस्तपोमूर्तिमिमं मन्त्रमुदाहरेत्॥ १०॥

ॐ हरिर्विदध्यान्मम सर्वरक्षां
न्यस्ताङ्घ्रिपद्मः पतगेन्द्रपृष्ठे।
दरारिचर्मासिगदेषुचाप-
पाशान् दधानोऽष्टगुणोऽष्टबाहुः॥ ११॥

जलेषु मां रक्षतु मत्स्यमूर्ति-
र्यादोगणेभ्यो वरुणस्य पाशात्।
स्थलेषु मायावटुवामनोऽव्या-
त्त्रिविक्रमः खेऽवतु विश्वरूपः॥ १२॥

दुर्गेष्वटव्याजिमुखादिषु प्रभुः
पायान्नृसिंहोऽसुरयूथपारिः।
विमुञ्चतो यस्य महाट्टहासं
दिशो विनेदुर्न्यपतंश्च गर्भाः॥ १३॥

रक्षत्वसौ माध्वनि यज्ञकल्पः
स्वदंष्ट्रयोन्नीतधरो वराहः।
रामोऽद्रिकूटेष्वथ विप्रवासे
सलक्ष्मणोऽव्याद्भरताग्रजोऽस्मान्॥ १४॥

मामुग्रधर्मादखिलात् प्रमादा-
न्नारायणः पातु नरश्च हासात्।
दत्तस्त्वयोगादथ योगनाथः
पायाद्गुणेशः कपिलः कर्मबन्धात्॥ १५॥

सनत्कुमारो वतु कामदेवा-
द्धयशीर्षा मां पथि देवहेलनात्।
देवर्षिवर्यः पुरुषार्चनान्तरा-
त्कूर्मो हरिर्मां निरयादशेषात्॥ १६॥

धन्वन्तरिर्भगवान् पात्वपथ्या-
द्द्वन्द्वाद्भयादृषभो निर्जितात्मा।
यज्ञश्च लोकादवताज्जनान्ता-
द्बलो गणात्क्रोधवशादहीन्द्रः॥ १७॥

द्वैपायनो भगवानप्रबोधा-
द्बुद्धस्तु पाषण्डगणप्रमादात्।
कल्किः कले कालमलात् प्रपातु
धर्मावनायोरुकृतावतारः॥ १८॥

मां केशवो गदया प्रातरव्या-
द्गोविन्द आसङ्गवमात्तवेणुः।
नारायणः प्राह्ण उदात्तशक्ति-
र्मध्यन्दिने विष्णुररिन्दमः स्यात्॥ १९॥

देवोऽपराह्णे मधुहोग्रधन्वा
सायं त्रिधामावतु माधवो माम्।
दोषे हृषीकेश उतार्धरात्रे
प्रात्यूषेऽव्यादच्युतो मे सुचक्रः॥ २०॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे षष्ठस्कन्धे अष्टमोऽध्याये श्री नारायणकवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narayana Kavacham ||

|| Atha Shrimadbhagavatantargatam Narayanakavacham ||

Rajovaacha —
Yaya guptah sahasrakshah savahan ripu-sainikan |
Kridanniva vinirjitya trilokya bubhuje shriyam || 1 ||

Bhagavans-tan-mamakhyahi varma narayanatmakam |
Yatha-atatayinah shatrun yena gupto-ajayan mridhe || 2 ||

Shri Vishvarupa Uvacha —
Dhautanghri-panir-achamya sapavitra udanmukhah |
Krita-svanga-karanyaso mantrabhyam vagyatah shuchih || 3 ||

Narayana-param varma sannahyed-bhaya agate |
Padayor-janunor-urvor-udare hridya-thorasi || 4 ||

Mukhe shirasy-anupurvyad-onkaradini vinyaset |
Om namo narayanayeti viparyayam-athapi va || 5 ||

Karanyasam tatah kuryad-dvadasakshara-vidyaya |
Pranavadi-yakarantam-anguly-angushtaparvasu || 6 ||

Nyased-dhridaya onkaram vikaram-anu murdhani |
Sha-karam tu bhruvor-madhye na-karam shikhaya dishet || 7 ||

Ve-karam netrayor-yunjyan-na-karam sarva-sandhishu |
Ma-karam-astram-uddishya mantra-murtir-bhaved-budhah || 8 ||

Savisargam phad-antam tat sarva-dikshu vinirdishet |
Om Vishnave nama iti || 9 ||

Atmanam paramam dhyayed-dhyeyam shat-shaktibhir-yutam |
Vidya-tejas-tapo-murtim-imam mantram-udaharet || 10 ||

Om harir-vidadhyan-mama sarva-raksham
nyastanghri-padmah patagenra-prishthe |
Darari-charmasigadeshu-chapa-
pashan dadhano-ashtaguno-ashtabahuh || 11 ||

Jaleshu mam rakshatu matsya-murtir
yadoganebhyo varunasya pashat |
Sthaleshu mayavatu-vamano-avyat
trivikramah khe-avatu vishvarupah || 12 ||

Durgeshu-atavyaji-mukhadishu prabhuh
payan nrisimho-asura-yuthpaparih |
Vimunchato yasya maha-attahasam
disho vinedur-nyapatamshcha garbhah || 13 ||

Rakshatu-asau madhvani yajna-kalpah
sva-damshtrayonnita-dharo varahah |
Ramo-adri-kuteshvatha vipravase
sa-lakshmano-avyad-bharatagrajo-asman || 14 ||

Mam-ugra-dharmad-akhilat pramadan
narayanah patu narashcha hasat |
Dattas-tvayogad-atha yoganathah
payad-guneshah kapilah karma-bandhat || 15 ||

Sanatkumaro vatu kamadeva-
d-dhayashirsha mam pathi devahelanat |
Devarshi-varyah purusharcha-nantara-
t-kurmo harir mam nirayad-asheshat || 16 ||

Dhanvantarir-bhagavan patva-pathyad
dvandvad-bhayad-rishabho nirjitatma |
Yajnash-cha lokad-avataj-janantat
balo ganat-krodhavashad-ahindrah || 17 ||

Dvaipayano bhagavan-aprabhodha-
d-buddhas-tu pashandagana-pramadat |
Kalkih kale kalamalam prapad-tu
dharmavana-yoru-krita-avatarah || 18 ||

Mam Keshavo gadaya pratar-avyad
Govinda asangavam-atta-venuh |
Narayanah prahna udatta-shaktir
madhyandine Vishnur-arindamah syat || 19 ||

Devo-aparahne Madhuho-ugra-dhanva
sayam Tridhama-avatu Madhavo mam |
Doshe Hrishikesha uta-ardha-ratre
pratyushe-avyad-Achyuto me suchakrah || 20 ||

|| Iti Shrimadbhagavate Mahapurane Shashthaskhande Ashtamo-adhyaye Shri Narayanakavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
राजा इन्द्र ने कहा — जिस कवच से सुरक्षित होकर सहस्राक्ष इन्द्र ने अपने वाहनों सहित शत्रु सैनिकों को खेल-खेल में जीतकर तीनों लोकों की सम्पत्ति का उपभोग किया।

हे भगवन्! मुझे वह नारायणात्मक कवच बताइए, जिससे सुरक्षित होकर मैं आक्रमणकारी शत्रुओं को युद्ध में जीत सकूँ।

श्री विश्वरूप ने कहा — पैर और हाथ धोकर, आचमन करके, पवित्र होकर, उत्तर की ओर मुख करके, स्वांग और कर न्यास करके, मन्त्रों द्वारा मौन और शुद्ध होकर, भय आने पर नारायण परम कवच धारण करे।

ॐ — हरि मेरी सम्पूर्ण रक्षा करें, जो गरुड़ की पीठ पर अपने चरण कमल रखे हुए हैं, ढाल, तलवार, गदा, बाण, धनुष और पाश धारण किये आठ गुणों वाले आठ भुजाओं वाले हैं।

जल में मत्स्य मूर्ति मेरी रक्षा करें, स्थल में वामन रक्षा करें, आकाश में विश्वरूप रक्षा करें। दुर्गम स्थानों में नरसिंह रक्षा करें।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीनारायणकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य विश्वरूप ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् एवं त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीनारायणो देवता। ॐ नमो नारायणाय बीजम्। नारायणी शक्तिः। श्रीनारायणप्रीत्यर्थे नारायणकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides divine armor-like protection from all dangers
Shields from evil forces, black magic, and negative energies
Invokes victory over enemies and adversaries
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and instills courage
Invokes the protection of all avatars of Lord Vishnu
Protects during travel and in dangerous situations
Invokes the blessing of spiritual strength and divine grace
description: Narayana Kavacham — नारायण कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Vishnu. Attributed to Srimad Bhagavatam - Skanda 6, Chapter 8, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow spiritual strength and divine grace and grant victory over enemies and adversaries. It is also said to protect during travel and in dangerous situations. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi. At 20 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 20 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Narayana Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narayana Kavacham from Srimad Bhagavatam in Sanskrit with transliteration. Divine armor prayer to Lord Vishnu for protection.

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Narayana Kavacham found in scripture?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narayana Kavacham is preserved in the Srimad Bhagavata Purana, Skandha 6, Chapter 8, where it is taught by the brahmin sage Vishvarupa to Indra during the asura-deva conflict. Indra approached Vishvarupa, son of Tvashta, after being defeated by the asuras, and the sage imparted this armor of Lord Narayana along with the Sudarshana Chakra invocation as the supreme protection. Empowered by this kavacham Indra subsequently won back the heavens. The narrative context establishes the kavacham as a battle-tested protective formula, and it remains one of the most respected Vaishnava protective recitations in classical tradition. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 6.8; Stotra Nidhi; Vedabase.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Narayana Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacham unfolds in three concentric layers of protection. The first invokes the twelve Vishnu names — Kesava, Narayana, Madhava, Govinda, Vishnu, Madhusudana, Trivikrama, Vamana, Sridhara, Hrishikesha, Padmanabha and Damodara — to guard the twelve limbs and directions of the body. The second invokes ten avatars of Vishnu (Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha, Kalki) to protect against ten categories of danger. The third invokes the Sudarshana Chakra, Kaumodaki gada, Panchajanya shankha, Sharnga bow and Nandaka sword as personified weapons that destroy hostile mantras, asuras, ghosts, planets, diseases and untimely death. (Sources: Bhagavata 6.8; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narayana Kavacham phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhagavata phalashruti, spoken by Shukadeva to Parikshit, states that whoever wears this kavacham with faith — by recitation, by inscribing it on bark or copper as a yantra-amulet, or by simply hearing it — is freed from every fear including poison, weapons, fire, water, wild animals, planetary afflictions, ghosts, hostile mantras and untimely death. It is said to have protected Indra in war, restored a brahmin from death by a sarpa-stra, and granted progeny to the childless. The phalashruti specifically states that one who recites it daily attains both worldly success and final liberation in Vaikuntha. (Sources: Bhagavata 6.8; Stotra Nidhi; Vedabase.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Narayana Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the traditional weekday for Lord Vishnu, and Sunday is also auspicious for Surya-Narayana worship. Every Ekadashi, especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha, is a peak day for kavacham recitation. Devotees facing planetary afflictions, repeated illnesses, court cases or hostile environments are advised to recite the kavacham daily after the morning bath, facing east, with a Vishnu yantra or Shaligram before them. Many practitioners also recite it before journeys, before surgeries, and at the start of any important undertaking. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ekadashi; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Narayana Kavacham and Vishnu Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narayana Kavacham of Bhagavata 6.8 is the longer and more elaborate of the two, with about 35 to 40 verses covering twelve Vishnu names, ten avatars and the personified divine weapons. The Vishnu Kavacham, preserved in the Vishnu Purana and other sources, is shorter (typically ten to twelve verses) and follows the standard limb-protection format common to all kavachas without the avatar and ayudha sections. Both are recited in Vaishnava households; the Narayana Kavacham is preferred for serious crises, Indra-style battle situations or yantra-amulet preparation, while the Vishnu Kavacham serves as a daily compact protective recitation. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Narayana Kavacham be worn as an amulet?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The Bhagavata phalashruti explicitly mentions that the kavacham may be inscribed on bhurja-patra (birch bark) or copper plate, consecrated by recitation, and worn on the right arm of a man or the neck of a woman or child as a kavach-amulet. This practice is documented in Vaishnava paddhati texts and is still followed by traditional acharyas in Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka. The amulet is typically prepared on a Thursday or Ekadashi after 1008 recitations of the kavacham by a qualified pandita. Even without the amulet, daily oral recitation is said to confer the same protective effect. (Sources: Bhagavata 6.8; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Narayana Kavacham appropriate for non-initiated devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a stotra preserved in the Bhagavata Purana — itself the most accessible of the Maha-Puranas — the Narayana Kavacham is open to all devotees who can recite Sanskrit phonetically or follow along in transliteration. No specific diksha or initiation is required for oral chanting in personal puja. The traditional preparations are simply cleanliness, an east-facing seat, a Vishnu image or Shaligram, and a clear mind. Initiation under a Vaishnava acharya is required only for the consecrated yantra-amulet preparation and for using the kavacham as a karma-kanda ritual prayoga for others. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Vedabase commentary.)
